candidate: genus-differentia-define-op (Axis-A substrate; FK-L3-3 family; kappa_B breadth-substrate lever)
_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record per docs/next/research-engine.md SS2.1, at registry/assessments/<id>.json alongside f2b-replicate, compression-census and g-series. This interprets a Tier-0 DETERMINISTIC COUNTING census (bead kernel-of-truth-8es lineage; interpretive bead kernel-of-truth-w6s2), NOT a frozen experiment: there is NO frozen SAP and NO verdict object — grep define in registry/experiments + registry/results-log returns nothing by design. Backing is the honesty-guard tooling-measurement rail (committed RUN-LOG + reproduce command + shard shas). The RUN-LOG asserts MEASURED counts only and explicitly forbids extrapolation to any benchmark (WiC / def-MMLU / kappa_B); the meaning of those counts is decided here. REFRESH 2026-07-10 (w6s2): this revision (a) anchors the interpretation on the pinned 0.7710 post-8es datum that docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md SS1c cites, (b) adds the COVERAGE-GRADIENT placement (where 0.7710 sits in self-authored ~1.0 -> friendly-NL 0.3542 -> propositions ~0.006 -> Mathlib ~0.000) and the EFFICIENCY-thesis BREADTH lens (0.7710 as a kappa_B breadth-SUBSTRATE datum that informs but does not gate the feasibility verdict), and (c) records for honesty the later same-axis re-run (0.8032, foreign-filler stub tier, 2026-07-10) without moving any conclusion off 0.7710.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4 and the kern Fable/Opus execution split: Opus ran define_census.py and reported mechanical counts + a diagnosed mechanical cause; what the coverage MEANS for the grounding thesis, the linter Axis-A, the efficiency-thesis breadth story, and N1-LB kappa_B is decided here and only here. Scope discipline: registry/assessments/define-op-census.json + registry/assumptions.jsonl (my reserved ASM-0090..0099 block, append-only) ONLY; audit-status.jsonl is central custody (lines reported to the coordinator for serial append); no verdict, experiment, correction, engine, or census-runner file modified.

run_log.note: current bytes carry THREE runs: pre-8es 0.5478, post-8es 0.7710 (the datum interpreted here), and the 2026-07-10 foreign-filler-stub re-run 0.8032. The prior assessment cited the RUN-LOG at 0.7710 time (sha 4e882242...); this refresh cites current bytes and reads them honestly.

census_output.note: committed output shows the pre-8es 0.5478 shard table + unresolved-cause tallies; the 0.7710 and 0.8032 tables live in the RUN-LOG (Opus did not re-serialise the txt each run).

measurement_refs.population: 17,211 minted logicalDefinition-bearing onto-obo concepts (GO 9,307 + SO 219 + MONDO 7,685)
measurement_refs.engine: tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py DEFINE op (genus-differentia lookup), post bead 8es (extractor resolves differentia relations + engine reads the resolved relation field) + endorsements loaded from data/axioms-definitional-v0/
measurement_refs.shard_note_0p8032: the 0.8032 re-run bumped corpusIdentityRoot 1adab65e -> 7ad7988f via the NCBITaxon/CHEBI label-only stub tier; the 10 prior shards were byte-identical. 0.8032 is a SEPARATE MEASURED point on the same internal-coverage axis, not a re-measurement of the 0.7710-pinned shards.

_surprise_note: No prereg claim.md / registered prior exists (Tier-0 substrate census), so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally this is the EXPECTED pattern predicted verbatim by coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md: (i) the genus-differentia define-op is 'the cheapest kappa_B lever ... it needs zero new ingestion' and GO's 9,303 logical defs were the named ready substrate (GO measured 1.0); (ii) SS0 'checkability is not vocabulary coverage — three legs must coincide'; (iii) MONDO's foreign fillers (HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon/HGNC) are precisely the Wave-A ingestion dependency held for later. All three predictions held; the on-trajectory confirmation is captured in interpretation, not a surprise flag.

interpretation.headline: 0.7710 internal define-checkability is a SELF-AUTHORED-SUBSTRATE maturity signal at the easiest end of the kernel's coverage gradient — the cheapest kappa_B lever now demonstrably works (GO + SO fully checkable, MONDO's residual a cleanly-diagnosed cross-ontology DATA-ingestion gap, not an engine limit) — and it is NOT a kappa_B number and NOT an end-task grounding claim. It measures at most two of the three checkability legs (record present + define-op resolves) on the SELF-AUTHORED minted substrate; the binding third leg (mapper parse of an external benchmark item) is untouched. Placed on the kernel's monotone coverage gradient (self-authored ~1.0 -> friendly-NL 0.3542 -> propositions ~0.006 -> Mathlib ~0.000), 0.7710 sits at the self-authored end and its very position is WHY it bounds nothing downstream: the gradient's monotone-decreasing shape means an internal-substrate number cannot upper-bound any external point. For the EFFICIENCY thesis it is a kappa_B BREADTH-SUBSTRATE readiness datum — it widens the class of propositions the kernel can attempt and makes the define-lane ready for a b-cov external census — but it INFORMS the breadth story without measuring breadth and does NOT gate the feasibility verdict (whose bottom lines rest on de-confounding f2b, the NL boundary, and the semantics core).
interpretation.measured_read: MEASURED post-8es: 13,270/17,211 define-checkable = 0.7710 (up from 0.5478 pre-8es). By shard: GO 9,307/9,307 = 1.0000 (byte-unchanged by 8es); SO 219/219 = 1.0000 (8es fully unlocked it from 18/219 = 0.0822); MONDO 3,744/7,685 = 0.4871 (from 103/7,685 pre-8es). The 3,941 MONDO ERR_DEFN_UNRESOLVED are ALL foreign fillers (HP / CHEBI / NCBITaxon references), i.e. the held Wave-A cross-ontology ingestion gap — deterministically diagnosed, 0 failures on unminted genus/filler. The pre-8es cause (differentia-relation shorthand absent from the 10-value alias table) is retired: 8es (extractor resolving relations + the engine reading the resolved relation field) was the lever, not the DEFINE engine, which was never the bottleneck. LATER SAME-AXIS MEASURED POINT (2026-07-10, honesty, does not move the interpreted datum): the foreign-filler stub tier (NCBITaxon 515 + CHEBI 39 fillers ingested label/prose-only) moved MONDO 3,744 -> 4,298 and the aggregate 0.7710 -> 0.8032; the residual 3,387 is MECHANICALLY diagnosed as HGNC gene IRIs (~2,450 defs, non-OBO HUGO registry needing a bespoke gene-track extractor — a Fable design call), HP (~504, restrictive-licence held), CHR chromosome bands (318, ownership decision), and a small clean-licence OBO tail (~114). Both 0.7710 and 0.8032 confirm the identical structural fact: the misses are DATA/ownership gaps with named routes, never engine refusals.

coverage_gradient_placement.gradient: self-authored internal ~1.0  >  friendly-NL m0b 0.3542 (molecules-v0)  >  proposition roll-up engagement ~0.006 (idea-B)  >  Mathlib ~0.000
coverage_gradient_placement.where_0p7710_sits: [MEASURED, in scope] 0.7710 is a SELF-AUTHORED-END number: it counts define-checkability of the kernel's OWN minted onto-obo definitional records. On the two fully-ingested self-authored shards it IS the ~1.0 regime (GO 1.0, SO 1.0); the aggregate 0.7710 is dragged below 1.0 ONLY by MONDO's un-ingested cross-ontology filler references — a data-ingestion gap, not a difficulty signal from the external world. So 0.7710 belongs at the self-authored (easiest) end of the gradient, NOT at any point further down it.
coverage_gradient_placement.why_it_bounds_nothing_downstream: [MEASURED discipline] The gradient is monotone-decreasing because each step to the right adds a HARDER, less-controlled leg: self-authored records (legs 1-2 satisfiable by construction) -> friendly external NL vocabulary (m0b, still just surface-form membership) -> real propositions engaged by the roll-up machinery (~0.6%, engagement not correctness) -> a hostile external formal corpus (Mathlib ~0). The measured 2-3-orders-of-magnitude drop from self-authored to every external point IS the empirical form of the three-legs discipline: the fall happens at the binding third leg (external item -> closed grammar). An internal-substrate completeness number therefore cannot upper-bound any external-corpus point; it can only say 'the substrate the external leg would draw on is mature'.
coverage_gradient_placement.framing_caveat: [STIPULATED: ASM-0090] The gradient is a cross-instrument QUALITATIVE framing device, not a single measured monotone curve: its four points measure DIFFERENT quantities (internal define-checkability of self-authored records; m0b content-word vocabulary membership; idea-B proposition roll-up engagement; Mathlib checkability) on DIFFERENT corpora with NON-COMMENSURABLE denominators. It is used ONLY to place 0.7710 at the easy end and to license the NEGATIVE inference above; it NEVER licenses quantitative interpolation between points or a single-decay-law claim connecting them. load_bearing:false — no conclusion here rests on the gradient being a real curve; the scope bounds rest on the independently-MEASURED three-legs discipline.

efficiency_thesis_breadth_read.frame: [READ] The efficiency thesis is ALIVE-NARROW (registry/assessments/f-efficiency.json; feasibility-synthesis.md SS4). Its evidence splits into DEPTH (does kernel machinery buy accuracy-per-cost on the covered slice — the f2b verifier-offload line, +0.1507 at cost_ratio 0.103, audited but attribution-confounded) and BREADTH (how much of a benchmark the kernel can even ATTEMPT — kappa_B). The define-op is a BREADTH lever: it adds a new checkable predicate CLASS (definitional retrieval + exact structural equality) to the closed grammar, widening the KIND of propositions the kernel can decide beyond the four prior ops.
efficiency_thesis_breadth_read.what_0p7710_contributes: [MEASURED, in scope] 0.7710 establishes that the INTERNAL substrate for this new predicate class is 77% live (GO/SO complete), so the define-lane is functional end-to-end on ingested records and READY to be pointed at a b-cov external census. It expands the POTENTIAL breadth surface (a new op with a mature internal substrate) — this is the records-only value coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS1 says ingestion delivers immediately, now MEASURED rather than asserted.
efficiency_thesis_breadth_read.informs_not_gates: [EXTRAPOLATION: ASM-0091, load_bearing:false] 0.7710 is a kappa_B breadth-SUBSTRATE datum, NOT a breadth measurement: it does not translate to any external kappa_B value (leg-3 untouched; the define-checkable slice is biomedical/domain-skewed, so WiC/general yield may be small or ~0 per design-kot-query-define-op.md SS4). It INFORMS the efficiency-thesis breadth story (the lever works, its substrate is mature) but does NOT GATE the feasibility verdict: feasibility-synthesis.md SS4 conditions the efficiency bottom line (ALIVE-NARROW) on de-confounding the f2b positive (human f2b-transfer / K-NULL), and the correctness bottom line on the NL boundary + the g2/g3/g8/g9 semantics core — NONE of which this census touches. This datum moves neither verdict; it characterises a substrate that a future breadth measurement would draw on.

licenses: [MEASURED] Axis-A substrate maturity: post-8es the genus-differentia DEFINE op — the plan's named 'cheapest kappa_B lever' — resolves 77.10% of the minted onto-obo definitional substrate into engine status=answer, WITH GO AND SO COMPLETE (1.0 each). For the linter's Axis-A consumer (proposition-level coverage: each clean definitional record moves a proposition from lattice class U toward G+/G- on its covered domain, coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS1), the substrate is 77% live and the define-op lane is functional end-to-end on ingested records.

[MEASURED] The residual is a DATA/ownership gap with NAMED mechanical routes, not an engine defect: at 0.7710 all 3,941 MONDO misses are un-ingested foreign fillers (HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon); at 0.8032 the residual 3,387 is mechanically resolved into HGNC genes (non-OBO, bespoke extractor), HP (licence-held), CHR bands (ownership), and a small clean-licence OBO tail. GO byte-unchanged + SO fully unlocked confirms the engine + alias table are complete where their referents are already ingested.

[MEASURED] 8es was the decisive lever: define-checkability moved 0.5478 -> 0.7710 from the relation-resolution change alone, with SO 0.0822 -> 1.0 and MONDO 0.0134 -> 0.4871. A clean instrument-improvement datum: the pre-8es number under-measured the substrate because of a shorthand-alias gap, not a coverage ceiling.

[MEASURED, breadth-substrate] The define-op adds a new checkable predicate class whose internal substrate is 77% live; this makes the define-lane READY for a b-cov external kappa_B census. It converts 'is the define-op lane functional on our records?' from open to MEASURED-yes (0.77, GO/SO complete).

[READ, grounding thesis] For the CORRECTNESS/grounding thesis this is a substrate-readiness result: the DEFINE op can decide the definitional propositions the kernel has authored, on the covered (biomedical onto-obo) domain, for 77% of the minted corpus and ~100% of GO+SO. It strengthens the claim that the kernel HAS a decidable definitional substrate to ground against — a necessary precondition for any downstream grounding/verifier win — without itself being a grounding win over any baseline (no model, no external item, no null in this census).
does_not_license: [NOT kappa_B] 0.7710 is INTERNAL define-checkability of SELF-AUTHORED minted records; it is NOT N1-LB kappa_B and must never be quoted as one. kappa_B (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) is the fraction of EXTERNAL benchmark items the define predicate makes checkable, and requires the binding THIRD leg — a mapper parse of a real benchmark stem into the closed query grammar (coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS0). This census measures legs 1-2 (record present + define-op resolves) on the substrate; leg 3 is untouched. The governing MEASURED warning stands verbatim: d-ext showed ~49% lemma-touch coexisting with 0% checkability — vocabulary/substrate coverage and item checkability are different quantities, and checkability is the binding one.

[NOT a gradient interpolation] 0.7710 does not sit on a measured curve with 0.3542 / ~0.006 / ~0.000; those are DIFFERENT instruments on DIFFERENT corpora (ASM-0090). The gradient licenses only the qualitative placement (self-authored end) and the negative inference (internal completeness bounds no external point); it never licenses reading a decay LAW from the four numbers or interpolating a 'coverage at intermediate difficulty'.

[NOT an end-task claim] No accuracy, no baseline, no null, no model is in this census. It says the substrate is decidable, NOT that grounding the substrate helps any task or moves kappa_B breadth on any benchmark. Any grounding-thesis or efficiency-breadth end-task claim lives in the f2-line / N1-LB / b-cov experiments, not here.

[DOES-NOT-GATE] 0.7710 does not gate the feasibility verdict. feasibility-synthesis.md SS4 rests the efficiency bottom line (ALIVE-NARROW) on de-confounding f2b (human f2b-transfer or the K-NULL ablation) and the correctness bottom line (UNDETERMINED) on the NL boundary (l3a-parse/a5-nl) + the g2/g3/g8/g9 semantics core. This census informs the breadth-substrate picture and moves neither verdict.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Domain-skewed and biomedical: the define-checkable slice is onto-obo biomedical (GO/SO/MONDO). Per design-kot-query-define-op.md SS4, def-MMLU is largely NOT biomedical and WiC is general-vocabulary, so the define-checkable YIELD on a general benchmark 'may be small or ~0'. The selection-effect + biomedical-domain disclosure is mandatory on any downstream number; 0.7710 does not extrapolate to any benchmark or to the general concept space.

[EXTRAPOLATION, not a premise] 'Wave-A ingestion (HPO/ChEBI/NCBITaxon) plus the gene-track extractor pushes MONDO toward GO/SO-level completeness, and internal define-checkability toward ~1.0' is the mechanically-named DIRECTION on the Axis-A substrate axis (ASM-0091), but the magnitude and exact residual after ingestion are unmeasured (foreign-filler references can themselves reference further un-ingested terms; the 0.7710 -> 0.8032 step measured exactly this, closing only the NCBITaxon/CHEBI subset). load_bearing:false; resolution_path = re-run define_census.py after each shard lands. It does NOT translate to a kappa_B trajectory (that needs leg-3 + b-cov over benchmark items).

[SEPARATION] Endorsement was licensed by SHARD MEMBERSHIP (subject field non-load-bearing for THIS count, per RUN-LOG boundary-stop #1). The production definitional endorsement subject convention is UNPINNED (staged records carry __PENDING_FABLE_SUBJECT_PIN__, fail-closed). This census's counts are valid as a define-op resolvability measurement; they do NOT presuppose the eventual subject convention. Pinning it is a design choice — boundary-stop.
interpretation.expected_pattern_read: YES — on-trajectory, not a surprise. The coverage-growth-ingestion-plan predicted every observed cell: (1) the genus-differentia define-op is the cheapest kappa_B lever 'with the most existing substrate', GO's logical defs the named ready set => GO measured 1.0; (2) checkability is three coinciding legs, ingestion supplies legs 1-2 not leg 3 => this internal number is high while kappa_B stays unmeasured; (3) foreign-filler / cross-ontology references are the ingestion gap held to Wave-A/B => MONDO's exact residual is HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon (and, at 0.8032, the non-OBO HGNC gene track). 8es converting the pre-8es 0.55 to 0.77 by resolving differentia-relation shorthands is the plan's 'exploits already-ingested logicalDefinition' lever behaving as designed.

trajectory_read.axis_a_internal_define_checkability: [EXTRAPOLATION, direction only — ASM-0091] Toward ~1.0 as the foreign-filler + gene-track ingestion completes: the residual is entirely un-ingested/un-owned fillers with named routes. The 0.7710 -> 0.8032 step MEASURED the first slice of this direction (NCBITaxon/CHEBI). Re-run the census after each shard to MEASURE the delta. This is the Axis-A substrate axis and it is realizable by ingestion/extraction alone (records-only), consistent with the plan's SS1 Axis-A characterisation.
trajectory_read.benchmark_kappa_b: [UNMEASURED, gated] Strictly separate and NOT implied by the internal number. kappa_B moves only with (i) the mapper parsing real WiC / def-MMLU stems into the define query (leg 3), (ii) the b-cov census (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) run over EXTERNAL benchmark items, and (iii) the WiC/def-MMLU data unblock (WiC 401-gated; def-MMLU unpinned — RUN-LOG boundary-stop #2). Internal substrate completeness is necessary-not-sufficient for kappa_B; the LB-GATE thresholds (kappa_B >= 0.10 headline / >= 0.20 banner-free) are evaluated on b-cov output over benchmark items, never estimated from this census.
trajectory_read.what_this_census_unlocks: [READ] It makes the DEFINE-op substrate READY for a b-cov run against the Tier-A lexical/definitional benchmarks (WiC, def-MMLU) — the breadth-substrate value. The next informative step is not more internal counting; it is the leg-3 / b-cov measurement over external items, which is a DIFFERENT census and is blocked on the benchmark-data unblock + the mapper-parse leg.

forks_resolved.fork: is the define-op engine the bottleneck, or the alias/relation-resolution layer?
forks_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED — the relation-resolution layer (bead 8es), not the engine: 8es moved 0.5478 -> 0.7710 with the engine byte-unchanged on GO; the residual is foreign-filler ingestion, not engine refusal. 0 failures on unminted genus/filler.

forks_resolved.fork: does internal substrate completeness imply benchmark kappa_B?
forks_resolved.resolution: NO — three-legs discipline (SS0): this census measures legs 1-2 on self-authored records; leg 3 (mapper parse of external items) is the binding, untouched quantity. Kept strictly separate; the coverage gradient's monotone shape is the empirical form of this separation.

NO — it INFORMS the efficiency-thesis breadth-substrate picture but the feasibility bottom lines (efficiency ALIVE-NARROW, correctness UNDETERMINED) rest on de-confounding f2b + the NL boundary + the semantics core, none touched here (feasibility-synthesis.md SS4).
YES — every cell (GO 1.0, SO unlocked, MONDO foreign-filler residual) was predicted by the ingestion plan; on-trajectory. The 0.8032 re-run reconfirmed the same structural fact.

questions_opened.question: Re-run define_census.py after each Wave-A shard + the gene-track extractor lands (A4 HPO, A6 ChEBI, A7 NCBITaxon, HGNC gene-track) to MEASURE the internal define-checkability delta as the foreign-filler gap closes; the trajectory toward ~1.0 is currently EXTRAPOLATION (ASM-0091). The 0.7710 -> 0.8032 step is the first measured increment.

The actually-informative next step for kappa_B breadth: run the b-cov benchmark-checkability census (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) with the define-op predicate over EXTERNAL WiC / def-MMLU items (gold-parse and mapper-parse counted separately). This is leg 3 and is a DIFFERENT census from this one; it is the only thing that yields a kappa_B number.
blocking: N1-LB kappa_B on the lexical Tier-A set

A bespoke gene-track extractor (not the OBO-filler stub pattern) is a Fable design call before this slice is checkable; it is the single largest remaining internal-coverage lever.
